raw_cv_text:
[CANDIDATE_NAME] 

[EMAIL] | [PHONE] | linkedin.com/in/mina-vaez | Toronto, Canada  

R&D Senior Scientist  
PhD-trained Biomedical Scientist with 5+ years of hands-on experience in biomaterials, polymer 
engineering, and medical device research and development. Proven track record in collagen and 
biopolymer-based material design, mechanical and biochemical characterization, and GMP/GLP 
compliance. Experienced in cross-functional collaboration, regulatory documentation, and project 
leadership from concept through validation.  

EDUCATION  

Ph.D. in Biomedical Engineering, Faculty of Dentistry, University of Toronto, Toronto • 2025                                                                                              
M.Sc. in Biomaterial Engineering, University of Tehran, Tehran • 2019                    
B.Sc. in Material Science and Engineering, Iran University of Science and Technology, Tehran • 2016   
SKILLS  

Biomaterials & Polymer Science: Collagen scaffolds, gelatin, CMC, hydrogels, electrospinning, material 
modification, degradation studies, and sterilization methods. 
Characterization Techniques: FTIR, SEM, AFM, XRD, viscometry, HPLC, mechanical testing, spectroscopy, 
and protein-ligand conjugation. 
Regulatory & Quality Systems: GMP/GLP, ISO 10993 familiarity, SOPs, risk assessment, documentation for 
Class I/II devices. 
Biological & Biocompatibility Testing: Cytotoxicity assays, ELISA, enzymatic activity, in vitro and ex vivo 
models. 
Project Management & Communication: Technical reporting, cross-team collaboration, mentoring, data 
presentation, and scientific publications. 
Tools & Software: GraphPad Prism, Origin, MS Office, ImageJ, SPSS, ChemDraw.                                 

EXPERIENCE 

Postdoctoral Fellow, Faculty of Dentistry, University of Toronto • 09/2025-Present    

•  Conducting advanced research on the application of artificial intelligence and machine learning models 
(CNNs) for microscopic and clinical images for automated feature extraction and pattern recognition to 
enhance diagnostic precision. 

•  Designing and executing mechanical and biochemical characterization studies of material–tissue 

interaction.  

Graduate Researcher, Bozec-lab, Faculty of Dentistry, University of Toronto  • [PHONE]   

•  Developed and optimized collagen-based scaffolds and biomimetic materials with enhanced mechanical 

stability and biocompatibility.  

•  Conducted FTIR, AFM, and mechanical testing to correlate material structure with biological performance.  
•  Managed biocompatibility and degradation studies, ensuring adherence to biosafety and GMP standards. 
•  Collaborated with interdisciplinary teams on therapeutic material design, ensuring compliance with 

biosafety and discovery quality standards. 

•  Mentored junior researchers in experimental design, documentation, and adherence to GLP and GMP 

standards. 

•  Led preparation of manuscripts, abstracts, and presentations, resulting in 8+ peer-reviewed publications 

and 5+ international conference presentations. (Google Scholar)  

 Regulatory compliance manager in Bonyan Darman Parsian Company, Iran • [PHONE] 

•  Oversaw regulatory submissions and compliance for Class I and II medical devices under GMP frameworks. 
• 
 Collaborated  with  national  regulatory  authorities  and  ensured  technical  documentation  met  ISO  13485 
requirements. 

Regulatory and Quality Specialist in [CANDIDATE_NAME] Parsian Company, Iran • [PHONE] 

•  Assisted in preparing regulatory submissions and technical documentation for medical devices. 
•  Tracked changes in national regulatory requirements and provided summaries to senior management. 

Graduate Researcher, University of Tehran • [PHONE] 

•  Developed core-shell electrospun NO releasing wound dressing for enhanced wound-healing.  
•  Presented research findings in nationally recognized conferences. 
•  Published paper in peer-reviewed journals through inter-institutional collaborations. 

HONORS & AWARDS 

•  University of Toronto Student Leadership Award (UTSLA) • 2024            
•  Travel award for CBS conference sponsored by Humabiologics Inc. • 2022                 
•  Wilson G. Harron 3 Minute Thesis Competition Award • 2021 
•  Dr. [CANDIDATE_NAME] Memorial Award • 2020 & 2021 
•  Faculty of Dentistry Harron scholarship • 2021-2023 

VOLUNTEERING & LEADERSHIP  

•  President, Graduate and Post-Graduate Dental Students Society (GPDSS) • 2023-2024 
•  VP Learning and Development, Graduate Super Women Engineers (GradSWE U of T) • 2023-2024 
•  Social Director, UofT Biomedical Engineering Students’ Association (BESA) • 2022-2023 
•  Treasurer, Graduate and Post-Graduate Dental Students Society (GPDSS) • 2022-2023 
•  Publication Co-Chair, Toronto Biomedical Engineering Conference • 2022 

TRAINING & CERTIFICATES 

•  Biomanufacturing for Advanced Therapies - Quality Control and Microbiology (CATTI & OBIO, University 

of Guelph) • 2025 

•  Good Clinical Practice (Novartis) • 2024 
•  Cosmetic Science and Ingredients Specialization (Olay) • 2025 
•  Business Fundamentals (mini-MBA) (by Graduate Management Consulting Association) • 2024 
•  Commercialization of regenerative medicine (by medicine by design) • 2023 
•  Atomic Force Microscopy trainer and mentor, CAMiLoD, University of Toronto •2021 
•  Certified technical officer of Medical Equipment Manufacturers by Iranian Medical Device Society • 2017 

PATENT 

Mina  Vaez,  [CANDIDATE_NAME],  [CANDIDATE_NAME],  “Generating  Electricity  by  Foot  Pressure  throughout 
Walking”, February 2010, Department of Companies Registration & Industrial Property. (IR-63685)
pitch_text:
[EXCEL SOURCE]: R&D Scientist
Regulatory & medical affairs
Clinical researcher
Application scientist
[CANDIDATE_NAME] 


[FILE: clarify_MINA VAEZ.pdf]
Document 1: Assignment 1 (Clarify) 

Question #1: My Ikigai 

My Ikigai is innovating safe, bio-functional solutions that bridge the lab and the market. 

The central theme of my career is "Translation." I don't just want to synthesize novel 
materials; I want to ensure they reach the people who need them. My passion lies in 
Biomaterials (specifically collagen and hydrogels) and Cosmetic Science. My talent is 
dual-faceted: I can engineer a complex scaffold at the nano-level using electrospinning , 
but I also understand the Regulatory Pathways (FDA/Health Canada) required to get it 
approved. 

+4 

The world needs therapeutic and aesthetic products that are backed by rigorous science, 
not just marketing. By combining my technical R&D skills with my regulatory experience, 
I create a lasting impact: ensuring that high-performance products (whether wound 
dressings or anti-aging skincare) are safe and effective. To align my career with this, I am 
targeting Pharma and Science-backed Skincare, where compliance and innovation must 
coexist. 

Question #2: My Ideal Day 

My ideal day is a balance of high-tech characterization and strategic oversight. 

It starts in the lab, where I am analyzing the surface topography of a new collagen 
formulation using Atomic Force Microscopy (AFM). I love the precision of visualizing 
material interactions at the nanoscale. 

By mid-day, I shift to "Regulatory Mode." I review the technical documentation for a new 
product line, ensuring our SOPs align with GMP and ISO 10993 standards. I enjoy 
identifying potential compliance risks before they become problems. The afternoon is for 
collaboration—perhaps mentoring a junior researcher on experimental design or 
discussing AI-driven diagnostic tools with my cross-functional team. The day ends with a 
sense of holistic progress—advancing the science while protecting the patient. 

+2 

Question #3: Job Titles 

1.  R&D Senior Scientist (Biomaterials/Formulation): This aligns with my PhD 

research in collagen scaffolds and my certificate in Cosmetic Science. It is my top 
choice for companies like Deciem or J&J. 
+1 

 2.  Regulatory Affairs Specialist / Manager: I have direct experience managing 

regulatory submissions for Class I/II devices. This role leverages my ability to 
navigate ISO 13485 and GMP frameworks. 

3.  Clinical Research Scientist: With my Good Clinical Practice (GCP) certification and 
experience in biomedical testing, I can lead the validation phase of new medical 
products. 
+1 

4.  Application Scientist: My broad skill set in characterization (SEM, FTIR, HPLC) 
makes me an ideal candidate to demonstrate complex technologies to clients. 

Question #4: S.M.A.R.T Goals 

●  Goal 1 (Career Placement): Secure a Senior R&D or Regulatory position at a target 

pharmaceutical or skincare company (e.g., Johnson & Johnson, Deciem) by 
August 2026. 

○  Measurable: Apply to 3 targeted roles weekly; leverage my OBIO/CATTI 

training to network with 2 industry leaders per month. 

○  Attainable: My hybrid background makes me a strong candidate for these 

specific sectors. 

●  Goal 2 (Technical Expansion): Complete a proof-of-concept project applying CNNs 

(Machine Learning) to biomaterial image analysis by May 2026. 

○  Measurable: Submit an abstract on "AI in Biomaterials" to a local 

conference. 

○  Realistic: This aligns with my current Postdoc research. 

●  Goal 3 (Leadership): Transition my current academic leadership roles (GPDSS 
President) into a professional mentorship role within a "Women in Science" 
industry network by December 2026. 

○  Measurable: Join one industry committee or mentorship circle. 
○  Time-bound: By year-end 2026. 

 
 

[FILE: pitch_MINA VAEZ.pdf]
Document 2: Assignment 4 (Pitch) 

Selected Job Offer Position: Senior Scientist, R&D (Personal Care / Biomaterials) Target: 
Deciem (The Abnormal Beauty Company) or Johnson & Johnson Key Requirements: 

●  PhD in Chemistry, Bioengineering, or related field. 
●  Experience with biopolymers, proteins (collagen), and formulations. 
●  Knowledge of GLP/GMP and regulatory standards. 
●  Ability to lead projects from concept to validation. 

The Elevator Pitch 

"Hi, [CANDIDATE_NAME]. I am a Biomedical Scientist who bridges the gap between molecular 
innovation and regulatory compliance. 

I have a PhD in Biomedical Engineering where I specialized in collagen-based scaffolds 
and hydrogels, giving me deep expertise in the very ingredients that drive 
high-performance skincare and wound care. But unlike many researchers, I also have 
professional experience as a Regulatory Compliance Manager. I’ve managed ISO 13485 
submissions and know exactly what it takes to bring a Class II medical device—or a 
bioactive cosmetic—safely to market. 

+2 

Currently, I’m expanding my skillset into AI and Machine Learning at U of T, looking for 
ways to use data to predict material performance. 

I’m interested in [Company Name] because you prioritize science-backed claims. I want 
to bring my ability to design novel biopolymers and my knowledge of global regulatory 
landscapes to your R&D team, ensuring your next generation of products is both 
innovative and compliant."

hard_skills_analysis.skill_name: Diplôme universitaire en chimie, biochimie ou immunologie

evidence.content_quote: Ph.D. in Biomedical Engineering, Faculty of Dentistry, University of Toronto, Toronto • 2025

hard_skills_analysis.reasoning: AUDIT REJET: The provided Ph.D. in Biomedical Engineering, while related, does not directly prove a degree in chemistry, biochemistry, or immunology. The fields are interconnected, but the claim and evidence do not perfectly align.

hard_skills_analysis.skill_name: Expérience dans les domaines médical et/ou pharmaceutique et/ou de la cytométrie en flux

evidence.content_quote: R&D Senior Scientist with 5+ years of hands-on experience in biomaterials, polymer engineering, and medical device research and development.

hard_skills_analysis.reasoning: AUDIT REJET: The quote implies related experience in the medical field through medical device research and development, but it does not directly address pharmaceutical experience or flow cytometry, making the evidence weak.

evidence.content_quote: Mentored junior researchers in experimental design, documentation, and adherence to GLP and GMP standards.

hard_skills_analysis.reasoning: The candidate has experience with SOPs, as they mentored junior researchers in experimental design and documentation, and ensured adherence to GLP and GMP standards.

evidence.content_quote: Proven track record in collagen and biopolymer-based material design, mechanical and biochemical characterization, and GMP/GLP compliance.

hard_skills_analysis.reasoning: The candidate has experience with GLP regulations, as they have a proven track record in GMP/GLP compliance.

evidence.content_quote: Tools & Software: GraphPad Prism, Origin, MS Office, ImageJ, SPSS, ChemDraw.

hard_skills_analysis.reasoning: The candidate has listed MS Office as one of their tools and software skills.

rhetoric_analysis.analysis_text: La structure de la présentation est claire et bien organisée, avec une introduction qui présente les objectifs et les compétences de la candidate. La partie 'My Ikigai' est particulièrement convaincante, car elle montre une véritable passion pour le domaine des biomatériaux et de la science cosmétique. Cependant, certaines phrases sont un peu longues et pourraient être simplifiées pour améliorer la clarté. La candidate utilise également des termes techniques qui pourraient être expliqués pour un public non spécialisé. La persuasion est forte, car la candidate présente des exemples concrets de ses réalisations et de ses compétences, et montre une confiance en ses capacités.

readiness_diagnostic.cot_reasoning: Le candidat présente des forces notables en termes de communication, avec un score de 9/10. Cependant, il lui manque certaines compétences techniques clés, notamment la maîtrise de l'anglais et du français au niveau C1. De plus, les alertes logistiques concernant le visa et la localisation du candidat nécessitent une attention particulière. L'analyse de la rétention a également rencontré des erreurs, ce qui rend difficile l'évaluation du risque de départ du candidat.
readiness_diagnostic.risk_assessment: Les risques de rétention et logistiques sont présents, mais ils peuvent être atténués avec des mesures appropriées. Le manque de compétences techniques peut être comblé par une formation ou un accompagnement. Cependant, les erreurs d'analyse de la rétention nécessitent une réévaluation pour déterminer le risque de départ du candidat.

interview_guide.question: Nous avons noté des contraintes (Visa/Lieu). Quelle est votre flexibilité réelle à ce sujet ?
Il nous manque des preuves sur : Anglais et français (niveau C1). Pouvez-vous nous donner un exemple concret ?
interview_guide.rationale: Vérifier si c'est un oubli du CV.
